description_long: This object is a rectangular tabletop radio receiver, primarily black in color with a slanted front control panel. The front face features a digital display screen with green illuminated numbers, a series of tactile buttons, and two rotary dials likely for tuning and volume control. The left side of the front panel has a speaker grille with horizontal slats, while the right side is occupied by the control interface. Red accent lines and labels are visible, adding contrast and aiding in the identification of controls. The top, sides, and rear are solid and unadorned, constructed from a hard, matte material, likely plastic or metal. The brand 'Uniden' is visible on the front panel, indicating a commercial or consumer-grade radio receiver suitable for urban environments such as offices, garages, or public service stations.
description: A black rectangular radio receiver with a slanted front panel featuring a digital display, buttons, dials, and a speaker grille. Red accents and the 'Uniden' brand are visible on the front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the solid, featureless left panel and the slanted front control area.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the digital screen, buttons, dials, speaker grille, red accents, and the 'Uniden' brand.
description_view_2: Side view showing the solid, featureless right panel and the slanted front control area.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain, unadorned back surface with no visible ports or features.
